report_name: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploys New RATs 'MiniUpdate' and 'MiniJunk V2' in Espionage Campaigns

threat_actors.name: Screening Serpens
threat_actors.description: An Iran-linked APT group conducting cyber-espionage campaigns targeting technology, aerospace, defense, and telecommunications sectors in the US, Israel, and the UAE. The group uses sophisticated social engineering and has developed new malware families, MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2.
threat_actors.goals: Intellectual property theft and espionage against strategic sectors to advance Iran's domestic military and technology programs.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The group's primary delivery vector is spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments disguised as job descriptions or other business documents.

attack_patterns.name: Employee Names
attack_patterns.description: The group conducts reconnaissance to identify and target specific professionals within desired sectors to make their social engineering lures more convincing.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Victims are tricked into opening malicious documents or running fake installers, which initiates the infection chain for malware like MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2.

attack_patterns.name: AppDomainManager
attack_patterns.description: The MiniUpdate RAT uses AppDomainManager hijacking to manipulate legitimate .NET applications at startup, allowing it to disable security mechanisms and inject malicious code into the memory of a legitimate process.

attack_patterns.name: Visual Basic
attack_patterns.description: Previous campaigns by Screening Serpens have involved the use of malicious macros written in Visual Basic as an entry point before deploying the main payload.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 RATs likely use standard HTTP/S for command and control (C2) communications to blend in with normal network traffic.

malware.name: MiniUpdate
malware.description: A new RAT deployed by Screening Serpens that uses a .NET technique called AppDomainManager hijacking to manipulate legitimate .NET applications for defense evasion and code injection.

malware.name: MiniJunk V2
malware.description: An evolution of Screening Serpens' existing toolset, this RAT likely features improved C2 communication and evasion capabilities.

identities.name: Technology Sector
identities.description: The technology sector in the US, Israel, and UAE was targeted by Screening Serpens for cyber espionage.

identities.name: Aerospace Sector
identities.description: The aerospace sector in the US, Israel, and UAE was targeted by Screening Serpens for cyber espionage.

identities.name: Defense Sector
identities.description: The defense sector in the US, Israel, and UAE was targeted by Screening Serpens for cyber espionage.

identities.name: Telecommunications Sector
identities.description: The telecommunications sector in the US, Israel, and UAE was targeted by Screening Serpens for cyber espionage.

infrastructure.name: Screening Serpens C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 to exfiltrate data and receive commands. It likely uses standard web protocols to evade detection.

indicators.name: Screening Serpens AppDomainManager Hijack via COMPLUS_Version
indicators.pattern:
title: Screening Serpens AppDomainManager Hijack via COMPLUS_Version
id: 2b8c1f9a-1e3d-4b8c-9a1d-8c1f9a1e3d4b
status: experimental
description: Detects the setting of the COMPLUS_Version environment variable, a technique used by the MiniUpdate RAT for AppDomainManager hijacking.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGicw2JZzrntvpfBA9ZIepA-OayNyDaI66LFFe0kzp-Tw7y1XVcGw_fHfhFLWgsft6wmfWKlvBbApK8I6cGTvFBiNWgvrq_GQwUe3ODFLEb6evNCOdOe89R2vUe-yOK80RoTgwSDrPYlUeBZ2sUawqzGLwE_ovKyXKePHLserEIwlAt
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1574.014
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\reg.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'add'
      - 'HKCU\Environment'
      - '/v'
      - 'COMPLUS_Version'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of reg.exe to set the COMPLUS_Version environment variable, a hunting hint for the AppDomainManager hijack technique used by Screening Serpens.

indicators.name: Screening Serpens AppDomainManager Hijack Registry Key Modification
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Screening Serpens AppDomainManager Hijack Registry Key Modification
id: 5d9e2a1b-3c7d-4f9a-8b1d-9e2a1b3c7d4f
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the AppDomainManager registry key, a technique used by the MiniUpdate RAT for persistence and defense evasion.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGicw2JZzrntvpfBA9ZIepA-OayNyDaI66LFFe0kzp-Tw7y1XVcGw_fHfhFLWgsft6wmfWKlvBbApK8I6cGTvFBiNWgvrq_GQwUe3ODFLEb6evNCOdOe89R2vUe-yOK80RoTgwSDrPYlUeBZ2sUawqzGLwE_ovKyXKePHLserEIwlAt
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1574.014
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|endswith: '\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\AppDomainManager'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modifications to the AppDomainManager registry key, a specific hunting hint for detecting the MiniUpdate RAT's persistence mechanism.

indicators.name: Suspicious .NET Compiler Execution by Screening Serpens
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious .NET Compiler Execution by Screening Serpens
id: 8a1b4c7d-9e3f-4a1b-8c7d-9e3f4a1b8c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of .NET compilers like csc.exe or cvtres.exe, which may indicate malicious .NET code compilation as part of the Screening Serpens infection chain.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGicw2JZzrntvpfBA9ZIepA-OayNyDaI66LFFe0kzp-Tw7y1XVcGw_fHfhFLWgsft6wmfWKlvBbApK8I6cGTvFBiNWgvrq_GQwUe3ODFLEb6evNCOdOe89R2vUe-yOK80RoTgwSDrPYlUeBZ2sUawqzGLwE_ovKyXKePHLserEIwlAt
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-30
tags:
  - attack.t1574.014
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\csc.exe'
      - '\cvtres.exe'
  filter_legit_path:
    Image|startswith: 'C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_path
indicators.description: Detects the execution of .NET compilers from unusual locations, a hunting hint for activity related to Screening Serpens.
